items.headline: Kawhi Leonard Trade to Raptors Halted Amid NBA Investigation
items.summary: A blockbuster trade sending Kawhi Leonard back to the Toronto Raptors from the LA Clippers is currently on hold. The NBA has launched an investigation into the Clippers regarding alleged salary-cap circumvention, delaying what was expected to be a major offseason move. The Raptors declined to assume the risk of potential penalties, leaving both teams awaiting the league's resolution.

items.headline: Donovan Mitchell Inks Massive 4-Year, $273M Extension with Cavaliers
items.summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers have locked in their star guard Donovan Mitchell, who officially signed a four-year, $273 million contract extension. Mitchell, a seven-time All-Star, agreed to the deal on Tuesday, securing his long-term future in Cleveland.

items.headline: LeBron James Still Undecided as Cavaliers, Warriors Vie for Superstar
items.summary: The NBA free agency landscape remains significantly impacted by LeBron James's ongoing decision. While the Cavaliers await his potential return, the Golden State Warriors are reportedly making a strong push to acquire both James and Anthony Davis, setting the stage for a dramatic end to the offseason's biggest saga.

items.headline: Timberwolves Dominate Pelicans in Summer League Opener
Hawks Cruise Past Spurs in Las Vegas
Hornets Sting Magic in Summer League Matchup
Wizards Edge Jazz in Thrilling Contest
Kings Handle Clippers to Close Out Day 1
Zyon Pullin Shines in Summer League Debut
items.summary: Minnesota Timberwolves guard Zyon Pullin delivered a sensational performance in his Summer League opener, recording a double-double with 24 points, 12 assists, and 8 rebounds in the win over New Orleans.

items.headline: Isaac Johnson's Impactful Summer League Debut for Sixers
items.summary: Former Hawaii star Isaac Johnson made a strong impression in his Philadelphia 76ers Summer League debut, coming off the bench to score 20 points on efficient 6-of-10 shooting (4-of-6 from deep) in just 20 minutes, leading the Sixers to victory.

items.headline: #1 Pick AJ Dybantsa Ties Blake Griffin's Summer League Debut Record
items.summary: Washington Wizards' top overall pick AJ Dybantsa exploded for 27 points and 7 rebounds in his Summer League debut, tying Blake Griffin's record for most points by a No. 1 pick in a Summer League game.

items.headline: Yaxel Lendeborg Impresses with Double-Double
items.summary: Golden State Warriors' first-round pick Yaxel Lendeborg showcased his all-around game with 21 points, 10 rebounds, and 6 assists in a dominant performance against Dallas.

items.headline: Las Vegas Summer League Kicks Off with High Expectations
items.summary: The 2026 NBA Summer League in Las Vegas officially began on July 9th, with all 30 NBA teams participating in games at the Thomas & Mack Center and Cox Pavilion. The league runs through July 19th and features exciting matchups with top draft picks and emerging talent.

items.headline: Jordan Clarkson Re-Signs with Reigning Champion Knicks
items.summary: Veteran guard Jordan Clarkson has agreed to a one-year, veteran minimum contract, returning to the reigning NBA champions, the New York Knicks. Clarkson will add depth and experience to the championship roster.

items.headline: Charles Bassey Joins Golden State Warriors on One-Year Deal
items.summary: Nigerian center Charles Bassey has secured another NBA opportunity, signing a one-year contract with the Golden State Warriors. This marks his sixth NBA season.

items.headline: Key Transactions Officially Completed on July 9th
items.summary: A flurry of previously reported free agent signings and trades became official yesterday as the July moratorium ended. Notable officialized deals include Tari Eason's five-year deal with the Rockets, Jusuf Nurkic to the Jazz, and several two-way contracts.

items.headline: Friday's NBA Summer League Schedule (July 10)
items.summary: Eight more Summer League games are slated for today, July 10th, across the Thomas & Mack Center and Cox Pavilion. Key matchups include Milwaukee vs. Miami (1 PM ET), Cleveland vs. Indiana (1:30 PM ET), and the Lakers taking on Oklahoma City (7 PM ET).
